summary: Creatine phosphate rapidly synthesizes ATP initially for brief periods; though not sustainable long-term, it surpasses all mechanisms due to unmatched immediate availability needed at onset of intense exercise. Anaerobic glycolysis follows as next fastest before reliance shifts towards slower aerobic processes post-glycogen depletion. The focus here should be solely on identifying which pathway delivers quickest results without considering overall duration or efficiency beyond those crucial early seconds when energy demands are highest. To summarize more accurately within your constraints would require omitting much detail regarding longer term metabolic adaptations discussed extensively above yet irrelevant given strict time frame
